description.en: The American bison is the heaviest land animal in North America, known for its massive head and shaggy coat.
description.zh: 美洲野牛是北美洲最重的陆地动物，以其巨大的头部和蓬松的皮毛而闻名。

anatomy.en: The American bison is a massive animal that can weigh up to 900 kilograms and stand about 1.8 meters tall at the shoulder. It has a huge, shaggy head with short, curved horns and a large muscular hump on its back. Its thick, dark brown fur keeps it warm during freezing winters on the plains.
anatomy.zh: 美洲野牛是一种巨大的动物，体重可达900公斤，肩高约1.8米。它有一个巨大而毛茸茸的头，长着短而弯曲的角，背上有一个大大的肌肉隆起。它厚厚的深棕色皮毛可以帮助它在平原的寒冬中保暖。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Bison are herbivores that spend most of their day grazing on grasses and sedges. They live in large herds and can run surprisingly fast, reaching speeds of up to 55 kilometers per hour. During mating season in summer, male bison charge and headbutt each other to compete for mates.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 野牛是草食动物，一天中大部分时间都在吃草和莎草。它们成群结队地生活，奔跑速度非常快，最快可达每小时55公里。在夏天的繁殖季节，雄性野牛会互相冲撞和顶头来争夺伴侣。

habitat_and_distribution.en: American bison once roamed the Great Plains of North America in herds of millions. Today they live mainly in national parks and protected grasslands in the United States and Canada. Yellowstone National Park is one of the most famous places where wild bison still roam freely.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 美洲野牛曾经以数百万头的规模在北美大平原上漫游。如今它们主要生活在美国和加拿大的国家公园和受保护的草原上。黄石国家公园是最著名的仍有野牛自由漫步的地方之一。

description.en: Brown bears are large, powerful predators distributed across North America, Europe, and Asia.
description.zh: 棕熊是大型且强壮的捕食者，广泛分布于北美洲、欧洲和亚洲。

anatomy.en: Brown bears are among the largest land predators on Earth, with males weighing up to 600 kilograms. They have a distinctive muscular hump on their shoulders that gives them powerful digging strength. Their fur color ranges from blonde to dark brown, and they have long, curved claws that can be over 10 centimeters long.
anatomy.zh: 棕熊是地球上最大的陆地捕食者之一，雄性体重可达600公斤。它们的肩膀上有一个独特的肌肉隆起，这给了它们强大的挖掘力量。它们的毛色从金黄色到深棕色不等，弯曲的爪子可以超过10厘米长。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Brown bears are omnivores that eat berries, roots, fish, and small mammals. They are famous for catching salmon as the fish leap up waterfalls during spawning season. In winter, brown bears hibernate in dens for several months, living off the fat they stored during autumn.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 棕熊是杂食动物，吃浆果、根茎、鱼和小型哺乳动物。它们最出名的就是在鲑鱼产卵季节，在瀑布旁抓住跳跃的鲑鱼。冬天时，棕熊会在洞穴中冬眠好几个月，靠秋天储存的脂肪维生。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Brown bears live in forests, mountains, and river valleys across North America, Europe, and Asia. In North America, they are commonly found in Alaska and western Canada. The grizzly bear, a well-known subspecies, lives in the Rocky Mountains and Pacific Northwest.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 棕熊生活在北美洲、欧洲和亚洲的森林、山脉和河谷地带。在北美洲，它们常见于阿拉斯加和加拿大西部。灰熊是一种著名的亚种，生活在落基山脉和太平洋西北地区。

description.en: Also known as cougars or pumas, these large, secretive cats are highly adaptable predators.
description.zh: 也被称为美洲豹，这些大型且隐秘的猫科动物是适应性极强的捕食者。

anatomy.en: Mountain lions are sleek, muscular cats that can weigh up to 100 kilograms and measure over 2 meters from nose to tail. They have tawny, golden-brown fur, small rounded ears, and a long, heavy tail that helps them balance while running and jumping. Their powerful hind legs allow them to leap as far as 12 meters in a single bound.
anatomy.zh: 美洲狮是体型光滑而肌肉发达的猫科动物，体重可达100公斤，从鼻子到尾巴长度超过2米。它们有着黄褐色的金棕色毛皮、小而圆的耳朵和一条长而粗的尾巴，帮助它们在奔跑和跳跃时保持平衡。强壮的后腿让它们可以一次跳远达12米。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Mountain lions are solitary hunters that stalk and ambush their prey, mainly deer. They are most active at dawn and dusk and can roam territories as large as 800 square kilometers. After a successful hunt, a mountain lion may hide its food under leaves and return to eat over several days.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 美洲狮是独居的猎手，会悄悄跟踪并伏击猎物，主要捕食鹿。它们在黎明和黄昏时最活跃，活动范围可达800平方公里。一次成功捕猎后，美洲狮会把食物藏在树叶下面，然后连续好几天回来吃。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Mountain lions have the widest range of any wild cat in the Americas, stretching from Canada to southern Chile. They live in mountains, deserts, forests, and even swamps. In the United States, they are most common in the western states.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 美洲狮的分布范围是美洲所有野生猫科动物中最广的，从加拿大一直延伸到南美洲的智利南部。它们栖息在山脉、沙漠、森林甚至沼泽地带。在美国，它们在西部各州最为常见。

description.en: Sea otters are marine mammals that use tools like rocks to crack open shells while floating on their backs.
description.zh: 海獭是海洋哺乳动物，它们在仰泳时会使用石头等工具敲开贝壳。

anatomy.en: Sea otters are the smallest marine mammals, weighing between 14 and 45 kilograms. They have the thickest fur of any animal, with up to 150,000 hairs per square centimeter, which keeps them warm in cold ocean water. Their webbed hind feet act like flippers, and their sensitive front paws are perfect for grabbing food.
anatomy.zh: 海獭是最小的海洋哺乳动物，体重在14到45公斤之间。它们拥有所有动物中最浓密的毛发，每平方厘米多达15万根毛，可以在冰冷的海水中保暖。它们有蹼的后脚像鳍一样好用，灵敏的前爪非常适合抓取食物。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Sea otters eat sea urchins, crabs, clams, and other shellfish, using rocks as tools to crack them open on their bellies. They often float together in groups called rafts and even hold paws while sleeping so they do not drift apart. By eating sea urchins, sea otters help protect kelp forests, which are important ocean habitats.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 海獭吃海胆、螃蟹、蛤蜊和其他贝类，会用石头当工具在肚子上把壳敲开。它们经常成群地一起漂浮，这叫做「筏群」，睡觉时还会手牵手，以免彼此漂散。通过吃海胆，海獭帮助保护了海藻森林，这是非常重要的海洋栖息地。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Sea otters live along the coasts of the northern Pacific Ocean, from California to Alaska, and across to Russia and Japan. They spend almost their entire lives in the water, preferring shallow coastal areas with kelp forests. Historically they were hunted nearly to extinction for their fur, and today their populations are slowly recovering.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 海獭生活在北太平洋沿岸，从加利福尼亚到阿拉斯加，一直到俄罗斯和日本。它们几乎整个一生都在水里度过，喜欢有海藻森林的浅海沿岸地区。历史上它们曾因毛皮被猎杀到几乎灭绝，如今数量正在慢慢恢复。

description.en: Raccoons are highly intelligent mammals, easily recognized by their bandit-like black face masks.
description.zh: 浣熊是高度聪明的哺乳动物，很容易通过它们像强盗一样的黑色面罩认出来。

anatomy.en: Raccoons are medium-sized mammals weighing between 5 and 12 kilograms. Their most famous feature is the black mask across their eyes and a bushy, ringed tail with alternating dark and light bands. They have extremely nimble front paws with five fingers, almost like tiny hands, which they use to open jars, doors, and even latches.
anatomy.zh: 浣熊是中等体型的哺乳动物，体重在5到12公斤之间。它们最出名的特征是眼睛上的黑色面罩和一条蓬松的环纹尾巴，深浅条纹交替排列。它们的前爪有五根手指，非常灵巧，几乎像小手一样，可以打开罐子、门，甚至门闩。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Raccoons are omnivores that eat almost anything, including fruits, insects, eggs, fish, and even garbage. They are mostly active at night and are famous for washing their food in water before eating it. Raccoons are very clever problem-solvers and can remember solutions to tasks for up to three years.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 浣熊是杂食动物，几乎什么都吃，包括水果、昆虫、蛋、鱼，甚至垃圾。它们大多在夜间活动，最出名的就是在吃东西之前把食物在水里洗一洗。浣熊非常聪明，善于解决问题，能记住解决方法长达三年之久。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Raccoons originally lived in the forests of North America, but today they have spread to cities, suburbs, and even parts of Europe and Japan. They are very adaptable and can make their homes in tree hollows, attics, sewers, or abandoned burrows. They are most common in the eastern and central United States.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 浣熊最初生活在北美洲的森林里，但如今已经扩展到了城市、郊区，甚至欧洲和日本的一些地方。它们适应性很强，可以在树洞、阁楼、下水道或废弃的洞穴中安家。它们在美国东部和中部最为常见。

description.en: The moose is the largest member of the deer family, famous for the enormous, flat antlers of the males.
description.zh: 驼鹿是鹿科动物中体型最大的，以雄性巨大的扁平鹿角而闻名。

anatomy.en: Moose are the tallest members of the deer family, standing up to 2.1 meters at the shoulder and weighing up to 700 kilograms. Male moose grow enormous palmate antlers that can span 1.8 meters wide and are shed and regrown every year. They have long legs, a drooping nose, and a flap of skin called a dewlap hanging under the chin.
anatomy.zh: 驼鹿是鹿科动物中最高的，肩高可达2.1米，体重可达700公斤。雄性驼鹿长着巨大的掌状鹿角，宽度可达1.8米，每年脱落后重新长出来。它们有长长的腿、下垂的鼻子，下巴下面还挂着一块叫做「肉垂」的皮肤。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Moose are herbivores that feed on leaves, bark, twigs, and aquatic plants. They are excellent swimmers and can dive underwater to eat water plants from the bottom of lakes. Despite their calm appearance, moose can be very aggressive, especially mothers protecting their calves.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 驼鹿是草食动物，吃树叶、树皮、嫩枝和水生植物。它们是出色的游泳者，可以潜入水下吃湖底的水草。尽管驼鹿看起来很温和，但它们其实可能非常凶猛，尤其是保护小鹿的母驼鹿。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Moose live in boreal and mixed forests across North America, Europe, and Asia. In North America, they are found in Alaska, Canada, and the northern United States. They prefer areas near lakes, ponds, and wetlands where they can cool off and find aquatic food.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 驼鹿生活在北美洲、欧洲和亚洲的北方针叶林和混交林中。在北美洲，它们分布在阿拉斯加、加拿大和美国北部。它们喜欢靠近湖泊、池塘和湿地的地方，可以在那里乘凉和寻找水生食物。

description.en: Coyotes are highly vocal and adaptable canines that can thrive in deserts, forests, and even cities.
description.zh: 郊狼是发声频繁且适应性极强的犬科动物，能在沙漠、森林甚至城市中繁衍。

anatomy.en: Coyotes are medium-sized canines that look like a smaller, thinner version of a wolf. They weigh between 7 and 20 kilograms and have pointed ears, a narrow snout, and a bushy tail with a black tip. Their fur is usually grayish-brown on top and lighter on the belly, helping them blend into their surroundings.
anatomy.zh: 郊狼是中等体型的犬科动物，看起来像是狼的缩小版，更加纤细。它们体重在7到20公斤之间，有尖尖的耳朵、细长的鼻子和一条末端为黑色的蓬松尾巴。它们的毛通常上面是灰棕色、腹部较浅，有助于融入周围环境。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Coyotes are omnivores that eat rabbits, rodents, fruit, insects, and sometimes garbage from humans. They are famous for their haunting howls, which they use to communicate with family members across long distances. Coyotes often hunt alone or in pairs, but may form small packs in winter when larger prey is available.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 郊狼是杂食动物，吃兔子、啮齿类动物、水果、昆虫，有时还吃人类的垃圾。它们最出名的是那令人难忘的嚎叫声，用来和远方的家人沟通。郊狼通常单独或成对狩猎，但在冬天有较大猎物时，可能会组成小群体。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Coyotes originally lived in the prairies and deserts of western North America, but have now spread to almost every corner of the continent, including big cities like Los Angeles and Chicago. They are one of the most adaptable animals in the world. They can live in deserts, grasslands, forests, mountains, and urban neighborhoods.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 郊狼最初生活在北美洲西部的草原和沙漠中，但如今已经扩展到了大陆的几乎每一个角落，包括洛杉矶和芝加哥等大城市。它们是世界上适应性最强的动物之一，能生活在沙漠、草原、森林、山脉和城市社区中。

description.en: The only marsupial found in North America, known for 'playing dead' when threatened.
description.zh: 北美洲唯一的有袋类动物，以在受到威胁时「装死」而闻名。

anatomy.en: Virginia opossums are about the size of a house cat, weighing between 2 and 6 kilograms. They have grayish-white fur, a pointed pink nose, small dark eyes, and a long, hairless tail that can grip branches like an extra hand. Females carry their tiny babies in a pouch on their belly, just like kangaroos.
anatomy.zh: 北美负鼠大约和家猫一样大，体重在2到6公斤之间。它们有灰白色的毛、粉红色的尖鼻子、黑色的小眼睛，还有一条长长的无毛尾巴，可以像多一只手一样抓住树枝。母负鼠把她的小宝宝放在肚子上的袋子里，就像袋鼠一样。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Opossums are omnivores that eat insects, fruit, small animals, and even ticks — a single opossum can eat thousands of ticks in a season, helping control disease. Their most famous trick is playing dead when they feel scared, rolling over with their tongue hanging out until the danger passes. They are nocturnal animals that are mostly active at night.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 负鼠是杂食动物，吃昆虫、水果、小动物，甚至蜱虫——一只负鼠一个季节可以吃掉几千只蜱虫，帮助控制疾病传播。它们最出名的绝招就是在害怕时装死，翻倒在地上伸出舌头，直到危险过去。它们是夜行动物，大多在夜间活动。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Virginia opossums live in woodlands, thickets, farmlands, and suburban areas throughout the eastern United States, Mexico, and Central America. They are very adaptable and often make their homes in tree hollows, rock crevices, or under buildings. Their range has been slowly expanding northward due to milder winters.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 北美负鼠生活在美国东部、墨西哥和中美洲的林地、灌木丛、农田和郊区。它们适应性很强，经常在树洞、岩石缝隙或建筑物下面安家。由于冬天变暖，它们的分布范围正在慢慢向北扩展。

description.en: Beavers are nature's engineers, famous for building dams and lodges in rivers and streams.
description.zh: 河狸是大自然的工程师，以在河流和溪流中建造水坝和巢穴而闻名。

anatomy.en: American beavers are the largest rodents in North America, weighing between 16 and 32 kilograms. They have a flat, paddle-shaped tail covered in scales that helps them swim and slap the water as a warning signal. Their large orange front teeth never stop growing and are strong enough to chew through tree trunks.
anatomy.zh: 美洲河狸是北美洲最大的啮齿动物，体重在16到32公斤之间。它们有一条扁平的、桨状的、覆盖着鳞片的尾巴，帮助它们游泳，还能拍打水面作为警告信号。它们大大的橙色门牙永远不会停止生长，而且强壮到可以啃断树干。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Beavers are herbivores that eat tree bark, leaves, roots, and water plants. They are famous for building dams out of sticks, mud, and stones, creating ponds that become homes for many other animals. Beaver families live together in dome-shaped lodges with underwater entrances that keep predators out.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 河狸是草食动物，吃树皮、树叶、根茎和水生植物。它们最出名的是用树枝、泥巴和石头建造水坝，创造出的池塘成为了许多其他动物的家园。河狸一家住在圆顶形的巢穴里，入口在水下，可以防止捕食者进入。

habitat_and_distribution.en: American beavers live in rivers, streams, ponds, and lakes across most of North America, from Canada to northern Mexico. They prefer areas with plenty of trees near water, which they need for both food and building materials. Their dam-building activities create wetlands that benefit many other species.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 美洲河狸生活在北美洲大部分地区的河流、溪流、池塘和湖泊中，从加拿大到墨西哥北部都有分布。它们喜欢水边有很多树木的地方，因为树木既是食物又是建筑材料。它们建造水坝的活动创造了湿地，对许多其他物种都有益处。

description.en: Named for their large, curved horns, these sheep are expert climbers in steep, rocky terrain.
description.zh: 因其巨大的弯曲羊角而得名，这些羊是陡峭岩石地形中的攀岩专家。

anatomy.en: Bighorn sheep weigh between 60 and 140 kilograms and are named for the males' massive curved horns, which can weigh up to 14 kilograms — as much as all the other bones in their body combined. Their hooves have a hard outer edge and a soft, grippy sole that works like a natural climbing shoe. Their brown fur with a white rump patch helps them blend into rocky mountainsides.
anatomy.zh: 大角羊体重在60到140公斤之间，因雄性巨大的弯曲角而得名，角的重量可达14公斤——和它们身体其他所有骨头加起来一样重。它们的蹄子外缘坚硬、底部柔软有抓地力，就像天然的攀岩鞋。棕色的毛皮和白色的臀部斑块帮助它们融入岩石山坡中。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Bighorn sheep are herbivores that graze on grasses, shrubs, and other mountain plants. During the mating season, males run at each other at full speed and smash their horns together in dramatic head-butting battles that can be heard from far away. They live in groups and use their excellent vision to spot predators like mountain lions from a distance.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 大角羊是草食动物，吃草、灌木和其他山地植物。在交配季节，雄性会全速奔向对方，用角猛烈地互相碰撞，这种壮观的顶角战斗在很远的地方就能听到。它们群居生活，用极好的视力从远处发现美洲狮等捕食者。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Bighorn sheep live on steep, rocky mountain slopes and cliffs in western North America, from southern Canada to Mexico. They prefer open alpine meadows near cliffs where they can escape from predators by climbing to safety. Different subspecies live in the Rocky Mountains, the Sierra Nevada, and the desert mountains of the Southwest.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 大角羊生活在北美洲西部陡峭的岩石山坡和悬崖上，从加拿大南部到墨西哥都有分布。它们喜欢靠近悬崖的开阔高山草甸，可以通过攀爬到安全的地方躲避捕食者。不同的亚种分别生活在落基山脉、内华达山脉和西南部的沙漠山地。

description.en: A medium-sized wildcat with a distinctive short, 'bobbed' tail and tufted ears.
description.zh: 一种中等体型的野猫，具有独特的短尾巴和有簇毛的耳朵。

anatomy.en: The bobcat is about twice the size of a house cat, weighing 6 to 14 kilograms. It has long legs, large paws, and a short tail that looks like it was cut, which is how it got its name. Its fur is tan to grayish-brown with dark spots and stripes that help it blend into the forest.
anatomy.zh: 短尾猫大约是家猫的两倍大，体重在6到14公斤之间。它有长腿、大爪子和一条看起来像被剪断的短尾巴，这也是它名字的由来。它的毛皮呈棕褐色到灰褐色，带有深色斑点和条纹，帮助它融入森林之中。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Bobcats are solitary hunters that mainly eat rabbits, hares, and small rodents. They are most active at dawn and dusk, sneaking up on prey before pouncing with a powerful leap. They mark their territory with scent and claw marks on trees.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 短尾猫是独居的猎手，主要捕食兔子、野兔和小型啮齿动物。它们在黎明和黄昏时最为活跃，悄悄靠近猎物后用有力的跳跃扑上去。它们用气味和在树上的爪痕来标记自己的领地。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Bobcats live in forests, swamps, deserts, and even near suburban areas across North America. They are found from southern Canada through most of the United States and into Mexico. They are one of the most adaptable wild cats in the world.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 短尾猫生活在北美洲的森林、沼泽、沙漠，甚至靠近郊区的地方。它们分布范围从加拿大南部到美国大部分地区，一直延伸到墨西哥。它们是世界上适应能力最强的野猫之一。

description.en: The pronghorn is the fastest land mammal in the Western Hemisphere, built for incredible speed.
description.zh: 叉角羚是西半球速度最快的陆地哺乳动物，天生为了惊人的速度而生。

anatomy.en: Pronghorns stand about 80 to 100 centimeters tall at the shoulder and weigh 40 to 65 kilograms. Both males and females have forked horns, but the male's horns are much larger. They have huge eyes for spotting danger far away and extra-large windpipes and lungs to take in lots of air while running.
anatomy.zh: 叉角羚肩高约80到100厘米，体重40到65公斤。雄性和雌性都有分叉的角，但雄性的角大得多。它们有很大的眼睛用来远距离发现危险，还有超大的气管和肺部，能在奔跑时吸入大量空气。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Pronghorns eat grasses, shrubs, and cacti. They can run at speeds up to 88 kilometers per hour, making them the second fastest land animal in the world after the cheetah. They live in herds and use their amazing eyesight to watch for predators like coyotes and wolves.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 叉角羚吃草、灌木和仙人掌。它们的奔跑速度可达每小时88公里，是世界上仅次于猎豹的第二快陆地动物。它们成群生活，用惊人的视力来观察郊狼和狼等捕食者。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Pronghorns live on the open plains and grasslands of western North America. They range from southern Canada through the western United States and into northern Mexico. They prefer wide, flat areas where they can see and outrun predators.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 叉角羚生活在北美洲西部的开阔平原和草原上。它们的分布范围从加拿大南部到美国西部，一直延伸到墨西哥北部。它们喜欢宽阔平坦的地方，这样就能看到并跑过捕食者。

description.en: Often called 'sea cows', these gentle giants slowly graze on underwater plants in warm coastal waters.
description.zh: 常被称为「海牛」，这些温和的庞然大物在温暖的沿海水域慢慢啃食水下植物。

anatomy.en: West Indian manatees are large, gray, barrel-shaped mammals that can grow up to 4 meters long and weigh over 500 kilograms. They have paddle-like flippers, a flat rounded tail, and small eyes. Their thick, wrinkled skin is often covered with algae, giving them a greenish look.
anatomy.zh: 西印度海牛是大型灰色桶状的哺乳动物，体长可达4米，体重超过500公斤。它们有桨状的鳍肢、扁平的圆尾和小小的眼睛。它们厚实多皱的皮肤上经常覆盖着藻类，让它们看起来带有绿色。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Manatees are gentle herbivores that spend six to eight hours a day eating seagrass and other water plants. They are usually slow-moving and peaceful, and they must come to the surface to breathe every few minutes. Mother manatees are very caring and stay close to their calves for up to two years.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 海牛是温和的草食动物，每天花六到八个小时吃海草和其他水生植物。它们通常行动缓慢而温和，必须每隔几分钟浮出水面呼吸。海牛妈妈非常爱护幼崽，会在幼崽身边陪伴长达两年。

habitat_and_distribution.en: West Indian manatees live in warm, shallow coastal waters, rivers, and springs in the southeastern United States, the Caribbean, and Central America. In winter, they gather near warm-water springs and power plant outflows because they cannot survive in cold water. They need water warmer than 20 degrees Celsius to stay healthy.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 西印度海牛生活在美国东南部、加勒比海和中美洲的温暖浅海水域、河流和泉水中。冬天它们会聚集在温泉和发电厂排水口附近，因为它们无法在寒冷的水中生存。它们需要水温高于20摄氏度才能保持健康。

habitat.text_zh: 温暖的沿海水域和泉水

description.en: Recognizable by their leathery armor shell, they are excellent diggers that feed mainly on insects.
description.zh: 以其坚韧的铠甲外壳而容易辨认，它们是出色的挖掘者，主要以昆虫为食。

anatomy.en: The nine-banded armadillo is about the size of a large cat, weighing 2.5 to 6.5 kilograms. Its body is covered in bony plates that form a hard, armor-like shell with nine movable bands around its middle. It has a long, pointed snout, big ears, and sharp claws made for digging.
anatomy.zh: 九带犰狳大约有一只大猫那么大，体重2.5到6.5公斤。它的身体覆盖着骨质甲片，形成坚硬的铠甲状外壳，中间有九条可活动的带。它有长长的尖鼻子、大耳朵和用于挖掘的锋利爪子。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Nine-banded armadillos are mostly nocturnal and eat ants, beetles, and other insects by digging them out of the ground with their strong claws. An amazing fact is that a mother armadillo almost always gives birth to four identical quadruplets! When startled, they can jump straight up in the air.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 九带犰狳大多在夜间活动，用强壮的爪子从地下挖出蚂蚁、甲虫和其他昆虫来吃。一个惊人的事实是，犰狳妈妈几乎总是一胎生下四个一模一样的四胞胎！受到惊吓时，它们会直直地跳到空中。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Nine-banded armadillos prefer warm, rainy areas with soft soil for digging, such as forests, scrublands, and grasslands. They are found across the southern United States, Mexico, Central America, and much of South America. They have been steadily expanding their range northward in the United States.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 九带犰狳喜欢温暖多雨、土壤松软适合挖掘的地方，如森林、灌木丛和草原。它们分布在美国南部、墨西哥、中美洲和南美洲大部分地区。在美国，它们的分布范围一直在稳步向北扩展。

description.en: Famous for their black and white stripes and their ability to spray a strong-smelling liquid for defense.
description.zh: 以其黑白条纹和喷射强烈气味液体进行防御的能力而闻名。

anatomy.en: Striped skunks are about the size of a house cat, weighing 2 to 5.5 kilograms. They have glossy black fur with two bold white stripes running from their head down their back and onto their fluffy tail. Under their tail are special glands that can spray a terrible-smelling oily liquid up to 3 meters away.
anatomy.zh: 条纹臭鼬大约有一只家猫那么大，体重2到5.5公斤。它们有光亮的黑色毛皮，从头部沿着背部一直到蓬松的尾巴有两条醒目的白色条纹。在尾巴下面有特殊的腺体，能将一种气味极其难闻的油性液体喷射到3米远的地方。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Striped skunks are omnivores that eat insects, fruits, eggs, and small animals. Before spraying, a skunk will stomp its feet and raise its tail as a warning. They are mostly active at night and spend the winter sleeping in underground dens, sometimes sharing them with other skunks to stay warm.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 条纹臭鼬是杂食动物，吃昆虫、水果、鸡蛋和小动物。在喷射之前，臭鼬会跺脚并竖起尾巴作为警告。它们主要在夜间活动，冬天在地下洞穴中睡觉，有时会和其他臭鼬共用洞穴来保暖。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Striped skunks live in a wide variety of habitats including forests, grasslands, farmlands, and even cities across North America. They range from southern Canada throughout the United States to northern Mexico. They are very adaptable and can make their homes almost anywhere with food and shelter.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 条纹臭鼬生活在各种各样的栖息地，包括北美洲的森林、草原、农田甚至城市。它们的分布范围从加拿大南部到整个美国，一直延伸到墨西哥北部。它们适应能力很强，几乎在任何有食物和藏身之处的地方都能安家。

habitat.text_en: Open areas with a mixture of habitats
habitat.text_zh: 具有混合栖息地的开阔地区

description.en: Gray wolves are highly social apex predators that live and hunt in family groups called packs.
description.zh: 灰狼是高度社会化的顶级掠食者，以被称为「狼群」的家族群体生活和狩猎。

anatomy.en: Gray wolves are the largest wild members of the dog family, weighing 30 to 80 kilograms. They have powerful jaws, long legs built for running, and thick fur that can be gray, black, white, or brown. Their large paws act like snowshoes, helping them walk on top of deep snow.
anatomy.zh: 灰狼是犬科中体型最大的野生成员，体重30到80公斤。它们有强有力的下颌、适合奔跑的长腿和厚厚的毛皮，毛色可以是灰色、黑色、白色或棕色。它们的大脚掌就像雪鞋一样，帮助它们在深雪上行走。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Wolves live in packs of 5 to 10 members led by a breeding pair. They hunt together to take down large prey like elk and deer, using teamwork and clever strategies. Wolves communicate with each other through howling, body language, and scent marking, and their howls can be heard up to 16 kilometers away.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 狼群通常由5到10个成员组成，由一对繁殖配偶领导。它们协同狩猎，用团队合作和聪明的策略捕捉麋鹿和鹿等大型猎物。狼通过嚎叫、身体语言和气味标记来相互交流，它们的嚎叫声最远可以传到16公里之外。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Gray wolves once roamed across most of North America, Europe, and Asia. Today they live in tundra, forests, mountains, and grasslands in remote areas. In the United States, they are found mainly in Alaska, the northern Rocky Mountains, and the Great Lakes region.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 灰狼曾经在北美洲、欧洲和亚洲的大部分地区漫游。如今它们生活在偏远地区的苔原、森林、山脉和草原上。在美国，它们主要分布在阿拉斯加、北落基山脉和五大湖地区。

description.en: These social rodents live in large colonies called 'towns' beneath the surface of the prairies.
description.zh: 这些群居啮齿动物生活在草原地表下被称为「城镇」的大型聚居地中。

anatomy.en: Black-tailed prairie dogs are chubby, tan-colored rodents about 35 to 43 centimeters long, including their short black-tipped tail. They weigh around 0.7 to 1.4 kilograms. They have small ears, dark eyes, and short but strong legs with sharp claws for digging complex underground tunnels.
anatomy.zh: 黑尾土拨鼠是圆胖的棕褐色啮齿动物，全长约35到43厘米，包括它们那条末端为黑色的短尾巴。体重大约0.7到1.4公斤。它们有小耳朵、黑色眼睛和短而有力的腿，爪子锐利，用来挖掘复杂的地下隧道。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Prairie dogs eat grasses, seeds, and small insects. They are extremely social and live in family groups called coteries within their large underground towns. They have a special alarm bark that can describe the size, shape, color, and speed of an approaching predator, making their language one of the most complex in the animal kingdom.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 土拨鼠吃草、种子和小昆虫。它们非常善于社交，在大型地下城镇中以被称为「家族」的小组生活。它们有一种特殊的警报叫声，能够描述靠近的捕食者的大小、形状、颜色和速度，这使得它们的语言成为动物界中最复杂的之一。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Black-tailed prairie dogs live on the short-grass prairies of the Great Plains of North America. Their range stretches from southern Canada through the central United States to northern Mexico. Their underground towns can cover hundreds of hectares and contain thousands of individuals.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 黑尾土拨鼠生活在北美大平原的短草草原上。它们的分布范围从加拿大南部穿过美国中部一直延伸到墨西哥北部。它们的地下城镇可以覆盖数百公顷的面积，容纳数千只个体。

description.en: A powerful and ferocious animal that resembles a small bear but is actually the largest land-dwelling weasel.
description.zh: 一种强大而凶猛的动物，外表像小熊，但实际上是体型最大的陆生鼬科动物。

anatomy.en: Wolverines are stocky, muscular animals weighing 9 to 25 kilograms, about the size of a medium dog. They have thick, dark brown fur with lighter stripes along their sides and a broad, rounded head with strong jaws. Their large paws spread wide like snowshoes, helping them move easily through deep snow.
anatomy.zh: 狼獾是粗壮而肌肉发达的动物，体重9到25公斤，大约有一只中型犬那么大。它们有厚厚的深棕色毛皮，体侧有较浅的条纹，头部宽而圆，下颌强壮有力。它们的大脚掌像雪鞋一样张开，帮助它们在深雪中轻松移动。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Wolverines are fierce and fearless, sometimes driving bears and mountain lions away from their food. They eat a variety of foods including carrion, small mammals, birds, and berries. They are solitary animals that roam enormous territories, sometimes traveling 24 kilometers in a single day to find food.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 狼獾凶猛无畏，有时会把熊和美洲狮从它们的食物旁边赶走。它们吃各种食物，包括腐肉、小型哺乳动物、鸟类和浆果。它们是独居动物，活动领地非常广阔，有时一天内会走24公里来寻找食物。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Wolverines live in cold, remote boreal forests, alpine tundra, and mountain regions of the Northern Hemisphere. In North America, they are found mainly in Alaska, northern Canada, and parts of the northern Rocky Mountains. They need large areas of wilderness with persistent snow cover and are very sensitive to human disturbance.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 狼獾生活在北半球寒冷偏远的北方针叶林、高山苔原和山区。在北美洲，它们主要分布在阿拉斯加、加拿大北部和北落基山脉的部分地区。它们需要大面积有持续积雪覆盖的荒野，对人类的干扰非常敏感。

habitat.text_zh: 北方针叶林和高山苔原

description.en: A stout, low-slung mammal known for its digging abilities and distinctive facial stripes.
description.zh: 一种粗壮低矮的哺乳动物，以其挖掘能力和独特的面部条纹而闻名。

anatomy.en: American badgers have flat, wedge-shaped bodies built close to the ground, weighing 6 to 12 kilograms. They have short, powerful legs with long front claws that can be up to 5 centimeters long, perfect for digging. Their face has a striking black and white pattern with a white stripe running from their nose over the top of their head.
anatomy.zh: 美洲獾有扁平的楔形身体，紧贴地面，体重6到12公斤。它们有短而有力的腿，前爪可长达5厘米，非常适合挖掘。它们的面部有醒目的黑白花纹，一条白色条纹从鼻子延伸到头顶。

ecology_and_behavior.en: American badgers are expert diggers that hunt ground squirrels, prairie dogs, and other burrowing animals by digging them out of their tunnels at amazing speed. They sometimes team up with coyotes to hunt together, where the coyote chases prey above ground while the badger digs below. They are mostly active at night and are known for being tough and fearless when cornered.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 美洲獾是挖掘高手，它们以惊人的速度将地松鼠、土拨鼠和其他穴居动物从洞穴中挖出来捕食。它们有时会和郊狼合作狩猎，郊狼在地面上追赶猎物，而獾在地下挖掘。它们主要在夜间活动，被逼到角落时以强悍无畏著称。

habitat_and_distribution.en: American badgers live in dry, open grasslands, prairies, and farmlands across western and central North America. They range from south-central Canada through the western United States to central Mexico. They prefer areas with loose, sandy soil that is easy to dig through.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 美洲獾生活在北美洲西部和中部干燥开阔的草原、大草原和农田中。它们的分布范围从加拿大中南部到美国西部，一直延伸到墨西哥中部。它们喜欢松软的沙质土壤，便于挖掘。

description.en: Equipped with thick white coats and special hooves, they easily navigate extreme alpine environments.
description.zh: 配备厚厚的白色皮毛和特殊的蹄子，它们能轻松在极端高山环境中穿梭。

anatomy.en: Mountain goats stand about 1 meter tall at the shoulder and weigh 45 to 140 kilograms, with males being larger than females. They are covered in thick, woolly white fur that keeps them warm in freezing temperatures. Their hooves have hard outer edges and soft, rubbery pads underneath that grip rocky surfaces like climbing shoes.
anatomy.zh: 雪羊肩高约1米，体重45到140公斤，雄性比雌性更大。它们全身覆盖着厚厚的白色羊毛，能在冰冻的温度中保暖。它们的蹄子外缘坚硬，底部有柔软的橡胶状肉垫，能像攀岩鞋一样抓住岩石表面。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Mountain goats eat grasses, mosses, lichens, and other alpine plants. They are incredible climbers that can scale nearly vertical cliff faces to escape predators like eagles and mountain lions. In spring, they travel to natural salt licks to get important minerals their bodies need.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 雪羊吃草、苔藓、地衣和其他高山植物。它们是令人难以置信的攀爬高手，能攀上几乎垂直的悬崖来躲避鹰和美洲狮等捕食者。春天，它们会去天然盐碱地舔食身体所需的重要矿物质。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Mountain goats live high up in the steep, rocky mountains of western North America, usually above the tree line. They are found from southeastern Alaska through British Columbia and into the northern Rocky Mountains of the United States. They prefer the most rugged, inaccessible peaks where few other animals can survive.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 雪羊生活在北美洲西部陡峭的岩石山峰高处，通常在树线以上。它们分布从阿拉斯加东南部到不列颠哥伦比亚省，再到美国北部落基山脉。它们偏爱最崎岖、最难以到达的山峰，那里很少有其他动物能够生存。

description.en: The red panda is a small tree-dwelling mammal known for its reddish-brown fur and long, bushy tail.
description.zh: 小熊猫是一种小型的树栖哺乳动物，以其红褐色的皮毛和长而蓬松的尾巴而闻名。

anatomy.en: Red pandas are slightly larger than a domestic cat, weighing about 3 to 6 kilograms. They have thick reddish-brown fur, a black belly, and a ringed tail that they use as a warm blanket in the cold. They also have a 'false thumb', which is actually an extended wrist bone used to grip bamboo tightly.
anatomy.zh: 小熊猫比家猫稍大一点，体重约3到6公斤。它们有厚厚的红褐色皮毛、黑色的腹部和带环纹的尾巴，在寒冷时可以当温暖的毯子用。它们还有一个“伪拇指”，实际上是一根伸长的腕骨，用来紧紧抓取竹子。

ecology_and_behavior.en: Despite being classified as carnivores, red pandas mostly eat bamboo leaves and shoots, along with some fruit and insects. They are excellent climbers and spend most of their lives high up in trees. They are mostly solitary animals and are most active at dawn and dusk.
ecology_and_behavior.zh: 尽管被分类为食肉动物，小熊猫主要吃竹叶和竹笋，也会吃一些水果和昆虫。它们是出色的攀爬者，一生中大部分时间都在高高的树上度过。它们大多是独居动物，在黎明和黄昏时最为活跃。

habitat_and_distribution.en: Red pandas live in high-altitude temperate forests with bamboo understories in the Himalayas and high mountains of southwestern China. They thrive in cold climates and steep mountainous terrain. Their populations are unfortunately declining due to habitat loss.
habitat_and_distribution.zh: 小熊猫生活在喜马拉雅山脉和中国西南部高山地区，那些带有竹林底层的的高海拔温带森林中。它们在寒冷的气候和陡峭的山地地形中繁衍生息。遗憾的是，由于栖息地丧失，它们的数量正在下降。

habitat.text_zh: 喜马拉雅及中国西南森林
